DateSep 2025 — Present

Combat Robots

Lead and Developer, Rice Robotics Club · Onshape, 3D printing, waterjet, machining, soldering

A series of antweight combat robots. Each new bot fixes the problems found in the previous one. I lead two 1.5-hour build meetings per week, organize internal competitions, and coordinate our participation in Texas events. Three bots so far.

Rev A

Sparky

Plastic antweight · Vertical spinner · Fall 2025

My first bot. It competed at a regional competition in Houston and lost badly, and nearly every subsystem needed rework. These are the changes I carried into the next builds:

Changes for the next build
  • Weapon: make it larger, open the shaft bore by 0.100" for clearance, add a weapon key, blunt the striking edge (too sharp and it chips instead of biting), reinforce the base, and use a 0.75" striking face
  • Balance: fix the 0.400" counterweight spacing and move the center of mass
  • Chassis: thicker base plate, fillets where wires pass through, and wheel covers
  • Wedge: make it smaller with a slight curve
  • Drive: add a rear skid so the front does not lift when accelerating, and move to 2.5" wheels
Fig. 1: CAD model
Fig. 2: Wiring diagram
Fig. 3: Assembled bot
Fig. 4: After the first match
Rev B

Goblin

Plastic antweight · Vertical spinner · Spring 2026

The second bot, built from Sparky's change list. I made the weapon bigger and the chassis compact, with less internal space so components would not shift around while driving, which was a problem on Sparky. Goblin competed at the Dallas competition. The compact layout went too far, though. It was so tight that fitting and servicing components at competitions was difficult.

Changes for the next build
  • Layout: make more internal space available. Components were too cramped to service
  • Balance: keep the weight low in the chassis for a stable orientation
  • Weapon: stronger support, and consider supporting the weapon on both sides
  • Driving: make the bot able to drive upside down
Fig. 5: CAD model
Fig. 6: Assembled bot
Rev C

Barbarian

Plastic antweight · Vertical spinner · Spring 2026

My most recent bot, built after applying the changes from Sparky and Goblin. The chassis is flat, which gives it better balance and enough room to fit every component comfortably, unlike Goblin. I also added wedges at the front for combat. It competed at the Rice battle bots competition and fought better than the earlier bots, winning a match. It could still drive after being flipped upside down, but in that position it was very vulnerable to attack, and it lost to a horizontal spinner.

Changes for the next build
  • Body: build a more resistant structure. The bot is exposed to attack when it is upside down
  • Armor: better protection against horizontal spinners
Fig. 7: CAD model
Fig. 8: Repairs on competition day
